Name: Memo is received, time/date stamped and acknowledged in Director's office by the Office Aide
Office Aide sends to the Director who minutes/instructs accordingly to officer responsible for related task
Office Aide dispatches minuted memo to officers responsible OR Office Manager or Administrative Assistant prepares draft memo and sends draft to Director for review
Output: Acknowledgment copy of dispatch/ dispatch register

Name: Director reviews, amends if need be and returns to Office Manager or Administrative Assistant for production of final draft OR Director finalizes, signs and dispatches electronically while copying office email to constitute file copy, for archiving by Office Manager and Office Aide
Output: Email notification/ acknowledgment copy of hard copy dispatch

Name: Office Aide/ Office Manager/ Administrative Assistant/ Director dispatches to destination (sometimes electronically) while ensuring acknowledgement.
In the absence of electronic acknowledgement, Office Manager or Administrative Assistant sends a reminder after 3 working days
Director checks completion of processes
Output: All processes completed or Director requests adjustment

Name: Memo is reviewed for receipt by Office Manager for hard copies OR Office Aide/ Office Manager/ Administrative Assistant/ Director performs same action for electronic receipt of request
Output: The memo is officially registered in DCP Register and in OneDrive incoming and outgoing folder.

Name: Memo without necessary supporting documents/wrongly transmitted is returned to Client Directorate OR Director performs same action for electronic receipt of request
Output: Within the Commission, document is returned by Office Aide. Outside the Commission, document is electronically returned by Office Aide.

Name: Memo with all supporting documents is received and acknowledged in incoming register by Office Aide or electronically by Director
Output: Within the Commission, document is returned by Office Aide. Outside the Commission, document is electronically returned by Office Aide.

Name: Office Aide submits memo to Director for action with summary of request Director acts on request/minutes memo to appropriate DCP Staff/destination. Director, when acting on request electronically, copies DCP office email

Office Aide, Office Manager, Administrative Assistant receives treated memo, registers, scans, and produces copy of files Office Aide, Office Manager, Administrative Assistant generates hard copy for dispatch and archiving or for archiving alone
Output: The memo is officially registered in DCP Register and in OneDrive incoming and outgoing folder.

Name: Office Aide archives hard/electronic copy
Output: The memo is officially registered in DCP Register and in OneDrive incoming and outgoing folder.

Name: Office Aide dispatches duly registered memo to destination
Output: The memo is officially registered in DCP Register and in OneDrive incoming and outgoing folder.

Name: Office Aide delivers memo to destination and obtains acknowledgement in outgoing register
Output: The memo is officially registered in DCP Register and in OneDrive incoming and outgoing folder.

Name: Director/Office Manager and Office Aide continuously review daily dispatch of documents.
Output: Outgoing correspondence is effectively dispatched to appropriate quarters

Name: Director receives from PPO Protocol soft copy of draft NV with supporting documents where necessary
Output: Note Verbale is received with supporting documents where necessary.

Name: Director receives soft copy of NV from Office Manager
Output: Note Verbale is received with supporting documents where necessary.

Name: Director reviews send back to PPO Protocol for production of final hard copy while directing Office Manager to provide reference number
Director receives final version for initialling
Output: Reviewed NV is received by Protocol/Office Manager

Name: Director dispatches initialled Note Verbale to Protocol through OA/ OM, Admin
Output: Initialled NV is received by Protocol.

Name: Office Aide / Office Manager obtain Stamp from Office of Vice-President, Director of Cabinet or External Relation
Initialled Note Verbale is dispatched to Protocol Division
Output: Initialled and stamped NV is available for dispatch to, and receipt by recipient.

Name: Director checks with PPO Protocol/Office Manager or requests acknowledgement copy from Protocol Division
Output: Initialled and Stamped NV is acknowledged by recipient.

Name: Administrative Assistant recalls approved dates for Statutory Meetings by …
Output: Administrative Assistant submits approved dates for Statutory meetings.

Name: Director reviews and returns for production of final draft and production of cover memo by OM/ Administrative Assistant
Output: Final draft of internal memo to management is ready for signature.

Name: OM submits final correspondence and relevant cover memo to Director for her signature
Output: Final draft of internal memo to management is ready for dispatch.

Name: Director sends preliminary version of correspondence intended for President's approval to Commissioner for review and the electronical copy to EA of Supervising Commissioner
Output: Proposed dates sent to Management for review and instruction

Name: Upon receipt of Supervising Commissioner's amendments, Director adjusts as necessary with the support of Administrative Assistant and Office Manager
Output: Document reflecting dates adjusted by Management is available.

Name: OM prepares cover memo to accompany final versions to be dispatched to Supervising Commissioner
Output: Cover Memos and Final versions are available.

Name: Director signs cover memos, while checking again accompanying final versions
Output: Signed cover memos and Final versions available to be sent to Management.

Name: Office Aide dispatches to Supervisoring Commissioner
Output: Signed cover memos and Final versions are dispatched.

Name: Director reviews all processes for completion with Office Manager, Office Aide and Administrative Assistant
Receive reaction from Management
Upon receipt of Management/Supervising Commissioner's reaction to the meeting dates, Director adjusts as necessary with the support of Administrative Assistant and Office Manager
Output: The dates are amended, where necessary

Name: Director amends and returns for production of final accepted dates and production of cover memo by OM/ Administrative Assistant
OM prepares cover memo to accompany final accepted dates to be dispatched to Supervising Commissioner
Output: Cover Memo to accompany final accepted dates is produced

Name: OM submits accepted dates and relevant cover memo to Director for her signature
Director sends final accepted dates for President’s information through Supervising Commissioner for review and the electronical copy to the EA of Supervising Commissioner
Output: Final accepted dates are dispatched to Supervising Commissioner with EA in copy

Name: Once President’s confirmation of of the dates is received, Director informs DCP colleagues
Director instructs Office Manager to prepare draft correspondence for Management to inform all Community Institutions of the approved dates
Output: Final Memos providing information are ready for signature.

Name: Final versions of memos accompanied by signed cover memo are dispatched to Management
Output: Final versions are in possession of Management.

Name: Director instructs HOD Conference to approach DCP colleagues and support Directorates for expression of needs
Output: HOD Conference receives instruction to collate all statutory meeting needs.

Name: Director instructs HOD Conference to prepare a draft budget reflecting the collated needs, memorandum of understanding on organization of ECOWAS meetings, the approved standard rates for goods and services, the rules of procedure for the governing bodies and the order of precedence

Actors: Director,       Office Manager,HOD Conference,Administrative Assistant,Office Aide
Name: Director reviews draft budget and where necessary returns to HOD Conference for adjustment.
Output: HOD Conference insert necessary adjustment where required

Name: The reviewed budget reflecting Director’s amendments is returned
Output: Pre final version is ready for submission to Management.

Name: Director instructs the Office Manager/ Administrative Assistant to send the pre-final version of budgets for Statutory Meetings to Management
Output: Management receives pre-final version of budgets for Statutory Meetings.

Name: Upon receipt of Management/Supervising Commissioner’s amendments, Director adjusts as necessary with the support of Administrative Assistant and Office Manager
Output: The pre-final version of budgets is amended

Name: Director amends and returns for production of final draft budgets and production of cover memo by OM/ Administrative Assistant
OM prepares cover memo to accompany final draft budgets to be dispatched to Supervising Commissioner
Output: Cover Memo to accompany final draft budgets is produced

Name: OM submits final draft budget and relevant cover memo to Director for her signature
Director sends final draft budget intended for President’s approval to Supervising Commissioner for review and the electronical copy to EA of Supervising Commissioner
Output: Final draft budgets are dispatched to Supervising Commissioner with EA in copy

Name: Director instructs OM/Administrative Assistant to prepare an electronic and modifiable version of List of Participants for circulation to all departments.
Output: The electronic list is ready for dispatch.

Name: Director reviews the List of Participants and instructs for any adjustment to be made.
Output: The electronic version of the list is circulated to all Directors for their nominations.

Name: Director receives the populated list and finalizes by the deadline indicated.
Output: The pre-final list is ready for review by Management.

Name: Director sends preliminary version of List of Participants intended for President’s approval to Commissioner for review and the electronical copy to EA of Supervising Commissioner
Output: Preliminary version of List of Participants is sent to Management for review and instruction

Name: Upon receipt of Management/Supervising Commissioner’s amendments to the pre-final List of Participants, Director adjusts as necessary with the support of Administrative Assistant and Office Manager
Output: The pre-final List of Participants is amended

Name: Director amends and returns for production of final List of Participants and production of cover memo by OM/ Administrative Assistant
Output: Final List of Participants is produced

Name: OM prepares cover memo to accompany final List of Participants to be dispatched to Supervising Commissioner
Output: Cover Memo to accompany final List of Participants is produced

Name: OM submits final List of Participants and relevant cover memo to Director for her signature
Director sends final List of Participants intended for President’s approval to Supervising Commissioner for review and the electronical copy to EA of Supervising Commissioner
Output: Final List of Participants is dispatched to Supervising Commissioner with EA in copy

Name: Once final approved List of Participants is received, Director instructs the Office Manager to circulate the List of Participants
Output: All recipients of correspondence are informed of their participation.

Name: Director instructs the Office Manager to send the List of Participants to Communication Directorate for circulation to Staff
Output: All staff approved for participation in the meeting are duly informed.

Actors: Director,Officer Manager,Administrative Assistant,HOD Conference,Head ECOWAS National Office
Name: Director instructs the Reproduction Unit to prepare badges for all participants for AFC and Technical Meetings respecting ECOWAS Commission corporates design/ designs to guide Host Government for Ministerial and Summit level meetings
Output: Instruction to prepare the designs is issued

Name: Director instructs all HOD to submit the schedule of duty of each DCP Staff member for Pre, during and post Statutory Meeting.
Output: All schedules of meetings are clearly indicated

Name: Director instructs PPO Protocol to provide list of recommended hotels and negotiated rates.
Director instructs HOD Conference to provide updated and active meeting registration link for insertion in Administrative Note.
Output: The updated and active registration link is received.

Name: Director instructs the Office Manager to prepare and send the draft memo to other support directorates (ITS, DAGS, Communication)
Output: The final memo to support directorates is dispatched.

Name: The Administrative Assistant and OM prepares the draft Administrative Note for delegates inserting the list of recommended hotels, ECODOCS Login details and updated meeting registration link and currency exchange rates and expected weather.
Output: The Draft Administrative Note is ready.

Name: The draft Admin Note is submitted to Director for review
Output: The Draft Admin Note is ready.

Name: Director instructs Administrative Assistant/OM to electronically dispatch reviewed and approved Administrative Note to delegates.
Output: Admin Note is dispatched to delegates electronically.

Name: Once approved meeting dates are received, OM prepares invitation letters and submits to Director - still within PeC’s Purview

Administrative Assistant referencing archived meetings on ECODOCS and translated draft agendas of upcoming statutory meetings, prepares meeting titles reflecting the correct edition in all 3 working languages.
Output: Meeting titles and dates in all 3 languages are prepared on DCP letterhead.

Name: Document reflecting approved meeting titles and dates is circulated to all relevant stakeholders (National office in host member state, Protocol Division, Conference Division, all staff of DCP, Communication Directorate)
Output: Relevant stakeholders receive the meeting title documents

Name: Director convenes initial preparation meeting with HOD and reviews preparations of all deliverables, Including any required liaison with support directorate and deliverables from Director's office itself
Director convenes meeting with all DCP Staff to review overall preparedness and readiness of Statutory Meetings
Output: General Staff meeting specifically on preparedness of statutory meetings hold.

Name: Director request (proposes) and receive dates for meeting with Local Organizing Committee (LOC) and sends signed MOU on the Organisation of ECOWAS Meetings to Host Government
Output: Dates for meeting with LOC is agreed upon.

Name: Director instructs Office Manager to prepare memos to invite Staff of the support directorates to participate in the meeting of the LOC, while forwarding to support directorates signed Memorandum of Understanding (Base document for collaboration for Host Government)
Output: Memo inviting support directorates is dispatched along with copy of signed MOU to appropriate quarters

Name: Directorate Conference and Protocol leads the ECOWAS Commission delegation to participate in the LOC meeting
Output: The meeting holds, MOU is reviewed, face recognition is achieved, exchanges of contact information is done.

Name: For Statutory Meeting outside HQ, Director approaches management for approval for DCP advance team to proceed for identification and inspection of venues in the member state hosting the meeting or Chairing the Authority - still within PeC’s Purview
Output: Management’s approval for deployment of advance team.

Name: Director reviews facility inspections report and makes recommendation to Management and DAGS for designation and engagement of service providers
Director invites Management to conduct final check of facilities and arrangements- still within PeC’s Purview
While meeting/event is ongoing Director monitors smooth functioning of all meeting pillars (payments, bills, documentation, accreditation, signature of final reports, etc)
Director supervises the dismantling of meeting

Actors: Director,DCP Staff,All Participants including Member States.
Name: Director receives analysis of feedback and assessment forms, and reviews DCP procedures accordingly.
Output: Measurable feedback is obtained and identification of best practices and areas of improvement.

Name: Review and aggregate reports
Receive request
Director receives instructions from Management to prepare contributions
Director requests Director's Office Support Staff to provide previous versions where it exists/prepare preliminary draft
Director requests elements from divisions where necessary in electronic version
Director directs Support Staff to merge all elements and prepare second preliminary draft
Director reviews second preliminary draft and dispatches to Support Staff/Office Manager/Documentation Division for formatting
Output: Reviewed second preliminary draft is formatted

Name: Director receives formatted version
Director forwards DCP contribution to appropriate quarters (electronic version or hard copy)
Output: DCP Contribution is dispatched to appropriate quarters

Name: All DCP contributions to reports are available and accurate
Output: All DCP reports are available and accurate

Name: Receive request
Director receives instruction from Management/Technical directorate
Director requests draft concept note/terms of reference from relevant HOD / Team Leads in implementation of approved budget/activity plan
Output: Instruction is given to HOD, and Team Leads.

Name: Director reviews draft received and instructed relevant HOD/Team Leads to prepare final version
Output: Final version of document is prepared.

Name: Director dispatches completed document to appropriate quarters
Output: Document is dispatched to appropriate quarters

Name: Director ensures all concept note and TOR relating to activities in the approved work plan is available
Output: The concept note and TOR are available.

Name: Director instructs Office Manager or Administrative Assistant to prepare relevant background information, notice of meeting, draft agenda and work program
Director reviews preliminary background information and supporting document, amending where necessary or issuing instruction to DCP Staff for finalisation.
Director directs Office Manager to dispatch Notice of Meeting, Meeting Link, Agenda, Work Program and any supporting documents
Output: Notice of Meeting, Meeting Link, Agenda, Work Program and any supporting documents are dispatched

Name: Director instructs Office Manager or Administrative Assistant to prepare action points for Director's/ HOD/Team Lead's own use
Output: Action points for Director, HOD and Team Leads are prepared.

Name: Office Manager or Administrative Assistant activates meeting recording and transcription on the virtual platform
Director moderates the meeting
Office Manager or Administrative Assistant listens to the recordings of the meeting and generates action points, and agenda of the next meeting, then sends to the Director for review
Output: Action point is generated, and agenda is drafted

Name: Director reviews the report of meeting, and action points to the Office Manager or Administrative Assistant
Director reviews number of action point treated
Director receives approved draft agenda from Management

Director sends approved agenda to ECODOCS Team for review of details of Status of Documents spreadsheet.  Submit Draft Agenda for Management's review and clearance Indicate document availability on ECODOCS to Management and facilitate session with Secretary General and President's Office to jointly review Status of Documents and Translation
Output: Status of Documents spreadsheet is reviewed on a continual basis.

Name: Director instructs Support Staff to continuously adjust Status of Documents in line with President's instructions
Output: Status of Documents spreadsheet is reviewed on a continual basis.

Name: Director instructs ECODOCS Team to format and upload documents in all three languages as against President's approval of agenda items Director continues to release documents as upload is completed by Support Staff
Output: Agenda documents are formatted and uploaded to relevant meeting page on ECODOCS platform

Name: Director remains available to instruct ECODOCS Team for replacement/removal of any uploaded document
Output: Documents are uploaded in line with DCP’s directives.

Name: ECODOCS Team checks that system has automatically archived all documents and reviews meeting identifying details in the backend
Output: Archived documents on ECODOCS are checked for inconsistencies in terms of meeting identifying details.

Name: Director checks the availability of all documents on the relevant ECODOCS page of the meeting.
Output: The fully populated page is available

Name: Conference division through DCP requests for the activities for the quarter from office of the President
HOD assigns a Conference Assistant to extract detailed information from the approved work plan
Assigned Conference Assistant draws up the draft quarterly calendar of events.
Output: Draft quarterly calendar of events produced

Name: Conference Assistant submits draft quarterly calendar to HOD for vetting
Output: Draft quarterly calendar of events submitted to HOD

Name: HOD sends draft calendar to the director for approval and circulates to other directorates
HOD allocates each conference assistance to a specific directorate(s)
Each Conference Assistant to liaise with his allocated directorate to confirm the meetings in the quarterly calendar and update with logistics (venue/ venue/ amount of days/number of participants)
Assigned Conference Assistant undertakes checks at end of every month and update calendar when necessary. collation continues
HOD receives instruction from Director to prepare budgets for all statutory /sectoral ministerial meetings
HOD instructs Conference Assistant to contact other DCP divisions/ support directorates involved, also client directorate to obtain all details necessary for budget preparation.
Conference Assistant prepares the draft budget and submits to HOD for vetting
HOD sends the prepared budget to Director for onward transmission to the technical directorate.
HOD receives approved memo/ meeting request form from Director
HOD minutes of the meeting request to Conference Assistant in charge of necessary logistics preparation
Conference Assistant completes the logistics request form as against the approved budget and submits it to Director through the HOD
Output: The logistics form received by DCP

Name: Director approves and sends to admin directorate
Output: DGAGS receives an approved logistics form

Name: Logistics requested received from General Administrationistration and deployed by Conference Assistant assigned to the meeting
Output: Conference Assistant receives information from the support service

Name: Conference Assistant requests and receives menu from identified caterer(s) and submits to HOD

HOD with participation of Conference Assistant calls for meeting with caterer to discuss identified service points, timings, food distribution, presentation, packaging, number of waITS Staff, waste disposal etc.
Conference Assistant holds duty station in the hall
Output: The Conference Assistant oversees the meeting

Name: Conference Assistant supervises Support Staff and monitors the smooth running of the meeting
Output: The Conference Assistant makes sure everything provided is in line with what was requested

Name: Conference Assistant supervises food and ensures timely delivery of food to meeting venue and informs focal persons whenever food is ready
Output: The Conference Assistant makes sure everything provided is in line with what was requested

Conference Assistant presents and hands over to HOD for onward transmission to Director

Reprographic Conference Officer, Conference Assistants ensure that all hired equipment is released to the service providers

Conference Assistant submits meeting feedback form to HOD and receives completed form for dispatch to HOD for onward transmission to Director.

Officer receives approved meeting/transportation request from supervisor.
Officer makes hotel reservation for participants or delegates

For Road Transportation, Officer completes the transport request forms and submits to the General Administrationistration Directorate for official vehicle assignment.
Output: Filled and approved transportation request form

Name: For Ferry or rail transportation, Officer confirms travel schedules, requirements (conditions for travel) and cost of travel before communicating to client and processing the purchase of Ferry/Train Tickets
Output: Transportation arrangement is confirmed and communicated to delegates

Name: Officer arranges transportation of participants to the station and to the intended destination
Officer follows up with Travel Unit to obtain their travel itineraries.

Reception and Departure Ceremonies RECEPTION: Confirm availability of placards and Flap Jackets for Protcol Officer to wear to the airport for proper identification of Protocol Officer. Be at the Airport latest, 30mins before scheduled arrival of guests. Stand at the designated area holding the banner/placard while vehicle is positioned ready to receive the guests. Guests are received and ushered to the vehicle while ensuring at least 1 Officer is always at the designated point to receive the guests If the guest does not show up, the client directorate is informed accordingly to figure out what might have happened while the delegates received are appealed to, to wait for 10 mins to confirm location of the delegates yet to be received. Protocol approaches MFA Staff for support to receive high level delegates.
Output: Delegates are received and conveyed to the designated accommodation

Language Coordinator (LC) shall minute request to the Language Assistant (LA) for initial assessment

LA shall assess the validity of the interpretation service request & adequacy of client’s supporting documents (indication of budget/sheet, PR number where client is funding, working documents, venue, working languages, and the number of participants, need for SIE etc.)

Name: LA shall submit the assessed request to LC
When the request meets requirements, it is validated to by LC

Based on the interpretation service request, LC shall determine language combination, team strengths and SIE or virtual meeting needs
LC shall check availability of staff Interpreters and assign a team accordingly if they are available.

LA shall acknowledge receipt of the request, inform the client directorate about the action taken, on the instruction of LC give the team leader's contact, request for working documents and any other information.
When staff interpreters are not available and there are adequate budgetary provisions, LC shall instruct LA to send out availability requests to Freelancers as designated by LC
Proposed weighted team of interpreters based on response to availability checks sent. to LA (who sends this to the LA? I thought it is the other way round).
LC shall instruct LA to send out confirmations of offers to freelancers who confirmed their availability
LC sends memo to DDCP for SIE procurement

LA shall send working documents (or give the username and password to access ECODOCS as far as statutory meetings are concerned) and useful links to interpreters concerned

Should ECOWAS rent a mobile SIE from a supplier, it shall be installed no later than the eve of the meeting, shall be functional and placed in a position where a clear view of the high table/projection screen is provided. In addition, it shall be compatible with the virtual meeting interface and interpretation platforms.

LC shall appoint team leaders for meetings. The most senior staff interpreter shall be the de facto team leader. In event that there is no staff interpreter on the team, a freelance interpreter designated by LC shall play that role

The team leader shall check if practical arrangements are in place for interpreters i.e., availability of water, working documents including presentations, compliance with the work programme, ensure that professional cordial relations are always maintained among colleagues, etc.
Where practical arrangements are not in place, the Team Leader shall draw the attention of Conference Division or whoever plays that role
Output: Practical arrangements checked and Notification to Conference

Team leaders on assigned meetings shall ensure that DCP feedback forms are distributed to the client directorate and randomly selected delegates from the various language groups and collected at the end of the meeting. Survey Monkey with acknowledgment of receipt could also be used to ensure that their feedback is being monitored.

Actors: Team Lead, Language Assistant, Language Coordinator
Name: Team leaders shall submit feedback forms to LC no later than 7 working days after the end of the meeting. LC may discuss them with him/her if need be.
LC shall take corrective actions on issues within his/her purview if need be
LC shall submit feedback forms to DDCP when there are issues requiring her attention.
LA shall send the meeting link received from the Conference Section to interpreters

A day before the meeting or during the pre-meeting tests, roles shall be assigned. Each booth shall appoint a Captain, preferably the most senior staff interpreter or a freelancer if there is no staff in the booth.
The Booth Captain shall create a WhatsApp group with all his team members including the DCP Co-host. S/he should prepare a roster (order of work) in consultation with her/his team members.

The Booth Captain is the interface between her/his booth and the DCP Co-host and Host. S/he shall report any challenge brought to her/his attention or that s/he may have observed and that may adversely affect the smooth performance of her/his colleagues

Each booth shall agree on their preferences as far as symbols are concerned (?: 5mn more; ?: end your sentence; handshake symbol: handover) or Messages (5 min before handover: “GET READY” and when it is time: “REPREND” or “GO”)
Upon handing over, the interpreter that was ON shall listen to the one who has taken over, from his/her second device, to ensure that the transition is going smoothly.

Interpreters' Cohost is designated by the Host (organizer of the meeting) in consultation with DDCP. S/he is the interface between the three Booth Captains and the Host. S/He shall perform sound check by listening to the various booths and confirming that the sound is loud and clear before the meeting starts. When participants log on, another sound check shall be carried out with them to ensure that they are good to go.
Output: Interpreters cohost designated Sound check conducted

Name: Sound check shall be led by DCP Cohost
LC shall instruct LA to inform colleague interpreters of the start time of sound check
Check that input and output sound are clear
DCP Cohost shall try to resolve challenges that occur and report those beyond his/her control to LC
In the event of serious difficulties that cannot be resolved at their level LC shall inform DDCP for appropriate action to be taken by ITS
The participants' audio check shall also be done by LC before any audio output is disseminated

Team leaders on assigned meetings shall ensure that DCP feedback forms are distributed to the client directorate and randomly selected delegates from the various language groups and collected at the end of the meeting. Survey Monkey with acknowledgment of receipt could also be used to ensure that their feedback is being monitored.
Team leaders shall submit feedback forms to LC no later than 7 working days after the end of the meeting. LC may discuss them with him/her if need be.
LC shall take corrective actions on issues within his/her purview if need be
LC shall submit feedback forms to DDCP when there are issues requiring her attention.

The team leader shall check if practical arrangements are in place for interpreters, i.e., availability of water, working documents including presentations, compliance with the work programme, ensure that professional cordial relations are maintained at all time among colleagues, etc. In addition, at the time of confirming the offer, the LC/team leader shall inform interpreters of any possible requirements to meet in respect of the virtual meeting platform to be used.
